title: Medication That Cures Too Well
logline: A biotech scientist announces a breakthrough drug that cures a chronic disease—then dies days later in a staged ‘accident’ as her company abruptly withdraws the product from the market.

setting.atmosphere: Sterile, high-stakes corporate biotech where billions of dollars hinge on clinical trial results and regulatory secrets.

victim.name: Dr. Aris Thorne
victim.cause_of_death: Carbon monoxide poisoning in enclosed lab garage (initially ruled accidental vehicle exhaust exposure)

culprit.motive: The breakthrough drug actually caused latent, fatal immune system cascades in 15% of trial subjects. Dr. Thorne discovered the CEO suppressed this data to secure a $2B acquisition by a pharma giant. She was preparing a whistleblower report to the FDA the morning she died.
culprit.motive_specificity_test: Only the CEO had the authority to alter the finalized clinical trial reports sent to the acquiring company and the FDA, and only the CEO stood to personally lose $400M in equity if the acquisition fell through.
method_steps: Accessed the sub-level garage control system using a master administrative override at 21:30.
Disabled the automated exhaust ventilation fans for Sector 4 where Thorne parked.

Used a modified remote starter frequency to keep Thorne's hybrid vehicle running its combustion engine while she was locked inside her nearby private lab office, filling the adjacent enclosed space with CO.
Deleted the HVAC override logs, making the fan failure appear as a routine mechanical fault.
culprit.the_almost: The CEO forgot that the building's secondary environmental monitoring system (used for clean-room pressure differentials) independently logs rapid CO2/CO spikes and cannot be edited by the standard HVAC admin account.

pois.name: Dr. Aris Thorne
pois.description: Brilliant but uncompromising researcher. Co-inventor of the drug. Had become increasingly isolated from management in her final weeks.
pois.hiding: She had secretly downloaded the raw, unedited trial data to a personal encrypted drive.

pois.name: Elias Vance
pois.description: Charismatic biotech executive driving a $2 billion acquisition. Obsessed with market timing and controlling the corporate narrative.

voice_profile.speech_pattern: Polished, uses corporate buzzwords, deflects specific questions with broad visionary statements.
voice_profile.under_pressure: Becomes overly precise and condescending; repeats the interviewer's name.

pois.surface_motive: Thorne was stalling the product launch, threatening the acquisition timeline.

alibi.claim: Working late in his top-floor executive suite, then took a private car service home at 22:00.
alibi.holes: He was in the building, but the car service log shows he didn't leave until 23:15, giving him ample time to stage the scene.

pois.hiding: He altered the Phase III clinical trial data to hide the fatal immune response rate.

pois.name: Dr. Julian Mercer
pois.description: Meticulous and cautious. Responsible for all FDA submissions. Known for being a rigid rule-follower.

voice_profile.speech_pattern: Dry, clinical, relies heavily on jargon and acronyms.
voice_profile.verbal_tics: Clears throat before answering difficult questions.

pois.surface_motive: Signed off on the flawed FDA submission. If Thorne blew the whistle, Mercer would face federal prison for fraud.

alibi.claim: At a pharmaceutical industry networking dinner downtown until midnight.
alibi.holes: Left the dinner early at 21:00 complaining of a migraine; unaccounted for during the time of death.

pois.hiding: He suspected the data was altered but chose to ignore it to protect his career and impending bonus.
pois.red_herring_reason: He drafted the urgent withdrawal memo before Thorne was even found dead, making it look like he knew she was going to die and preemptively pulled the drug.

pois.name: Sarah Chen
pois.description: Ruthless venture capitalist. Holds the largest external stake in Horizon and orchestrated the acquisition deal.

voice_profile.speech_pattern: Direct, impatient, speaks in bullet points. Interrupts frequently.
voice_profile.under_pressure: Threatens legal action, becomes icy and silent.
voice_profile.verbal_tics: Taps her ring on the table when irritated.

pois.surface_motive: Needs the acquisition to go through to save her struggling VC fund. Thorne's hesitation was a major roadblock.

alibi.claim: On a flight back from a conference in London.
alibi.holes: Flight landed at 19:30, giving her time to reach the campus before the murder.

pois.hiding: She was secretly negotiating to sell her shares to a rival company if the FDA delayed approval.
pois.red_herring_reason: She was seen arguing aggressively with Thorne in the parking garage earlier that day.

timeline.event: Thorne confronts Vance about the hidden Phase III data anomalies.
Chen and Thorne have a heated argument in the garage.
Mercer drafts the 'Urgent Product Withdrawal' memo.
Mercer leaves the industry dinner early.
Garage Sector 4 ventilation fans disabled via admin override.
Thorne succumbs to CO poisoning in her lab annex.
Vance leaves the building via private car service.
Security finds Thorne's body; HVAC system logs show 'mechanical fault'.

contradictions.what: Vance claims he left at 22:00, but the car service log shows 23:15.
contradictions.player_inference: Vance lied about his alibi and was present during the time of death.

contradictions.what: HVAC logs show a mechanical failure, but environmental sensors show a manual shutdown sequence.
contradictions.player_inference: The fan failure was intentionally triggered by an administrator, not an accident.

contradictions.what: Mercer claims the drug withdrawal was a reaction to Thorne's death, but the memo metadata shows it was drafted hours before she died.
contradictions.player_inference: The company knew the drug was doomed before the 'accident' happened.

evidence_chain.reveals: The death was caused by CO buildup due to a supposed HVAC failure.
The company planned to withdraw the drug before Thorne died, suggesting a massive cover-up.
Vance lied about his whereabouts during the crucial window of the murder.
The HVAC failure was a deliberate, admin-level override, proving murder.
Only Vance had the motive (the hidden data) and the admin access to execute the override.

A.description: Scene Setup — establish crime, introduce POIs, plant initial contradictions

B.description: Contradictory Evidence — interviews reveal lies, forensics deepen mystery

C.description: The Pivot — the document that breaks the case open, resolves main contradiction

R.description: Resolution — one document per POI with guilty/not-guilty reasoning + evidence refs

emotional_arc.envelope_A: Curiosity and unease — a brilliant scientist dies just as her life's work is pulled. What did she find?
emotional_arc.envelope_B: Suspicion and conflicting empathy — the corporate executives are all lying to protect their wealth, but who is capable of murder?
emotional_arc.envelope_C: Aha! and shock — the realization that the 'accident' was a calculated, digital assassination.
emotional_arc.envelope_R: Catharsis and satisfaction — the digital paper trail proves the CEO's guilt, exposing the massive fraud.

trojan_horse_docs.appears_as: A routine corporate expense report for executive car services.
trojan_horse_docs.actually_proves: Shows Vance's car waited from 22:00 to 23:15, destroying his alibi and placing him at the scene during the murder window.

trojan_horse_docs.appears_as: An automated clean-room environmental status alert email (spam).
trojan_horse_docs.actually_proves: Contains the raw sensor data showing a manual 'Admin-Override-Shutdown' command for Sector 4, contradicting the official 'mechanical failure' log.

social_media_plan.case_purpose: A biotech analyst noting the bizarre timing of the drug withdrawal and Thorne's death, planting the idea of a cover-up.
Vance's overly-polished memorial post for Thorne, which rings false and establishes his carefully managed public persona.
r/Biotech speculation about Horizon's Phase III data, hinting at the fatal immune response flaw.

spatial_tool.description: Schematic of Horizon Sub-Level 2, showing Thorne's lab annex, her parking spot in Sector 4, the HVAC ventilation zones, and the location of the CEO's private elevator access.

levels.label: Nudge
hints: Look closely at the timing of the drug withdrawal memo.

levels.label: Direction
hints: Compare Vance's interview statement about when he went home with the executive expense reports.

levels.label: Reveal
hints: The clean-room alert email shows the HVAC system didn't break; it was turned off by an admin.
